prevalence.source: IHME GBD / Brazilian psychiatric epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; improved detection and community follow-up in some cities

vulnerablePopulations: people with homelessness and social vulnerability
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: psychotic symptoms (delusions, hallucinations, disorganized thought) with functional impairment and duration criteria; rule out substance-induced psychosis and mood disorder with psychotic features

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis in primary care/ER with psychiatric referral
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; relapse common without adherence support and psychosocial services

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Psychiatric diagnosis typically in CAPS or hospital psychiatry; APS detects and refers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antipsychotics (typical/atypical) with psychosocial rehabilitation; long-acting injectables when available; family psychoeducation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Medication adherence, limited community rehabilitation services outside large cities, and social vulnerability barriers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS network designed for severe mental illness and community-based follow-up
